If the attack is successful, it renews in 1d6 rounds or after 3 strikes (option is decided once and applies thereafter).  If unsuccessful in removing that target from the fight, becomes available in 15 minutes or after 12 strikes (depending on option taken above).
Tinker: Death.  Pick one: Weak ranged weapon with limited shots/very slow rate of fire (ie.  every other turn max) is built into melee weapons, weak ranged weapon (as described above) is built into defensive gear, serviceable melee weapon is built into ranged weapons so they can be used in a sudden melee scrap without being dropped or exchanged for another weapon, or serviceable melee weapon (as described above) is built into defensive gear.  Weapons are not particularly advanced, and are simply shoulder mounted shotguns, ray guns, or suchlike, or wrist mounted blades, hammers, etc.
Thinker:
Death.  The thinker can better see how to put wounded targets down for the count.  They get a special execution move as appropriate that has an increased bonus against foes that have already rolled to stay in the fight.  Should this strike be unsuccessful, they lose this ability for the next three rounds.

As a secondary option, they gain increased damage against defensive foes, allowing their attacks to bypass resistances or armour in a minor but consistent way.

General:
Upright: Balance, moderation, patience, purpose

Reversed: Imbalance, excess, lack of long-term vision
Blaster:
Temperance.  Tenacity.  After every 2nd round of blasting, the blaster’s power gets a small boost, extending damage, accuracy against one of the defensive modes, effectiveness, environmental effects, etc.  Left free to keep delivering blasts, they get more and more dangerous.  The nature of the bonus that they ramp up with is decided as this is taken.  After 5 minutes of inactivity, they lose one step of boost.

Alternatively, every four rounds of blasting against hostile threats regenerates the blaster, keeping them in the fight.
Breaker: Temperance.  The shard refuses to let the cape fail.  This may manifest itself as a one-time-use check in order to stay transformed for a breaker, allowing the cape to suppress the effects on a single non-critical wound upon taking it, or another option via GM discretion.  As an alternate option, entering or leaving the transformed state (choose one) allows the healing of a small amount of damage, but prevents changing states again for a period of time.
Brute: Temperance.  Guts rolls to stay in the fight, including consciousness and death saves, gain +2.  In addition, the brute has a 25% chance to ignore any penalty to ability scores that would reduce an ability score to 2, a 50% chance to ignore effects that would reduce an ability score to 1, and a 100% chance to ignore effects that would reduce an ability score to 0.
Changer:
Temperance.  The shard refuses to let the cape fail.  This may manifest itself as allowing the cape to suppress the effects on a single non-critical wound upon taking it, or another such option as the GM dictates.

As an alternate option, entering or leaving the transformed state (choose one) allows the healing of a small amount of damage, but prevents changing states again for a period of time.
Master: Temperance.  Tenacity.  As combat progresses, minions gradually become more powerful.  Gains are typically small, but remain for the rest of combat.  Alternatively, every third round of combat, the master may distribute a small amount of healing between themself and their projections.
Mover: Temperance. The power refuses to let the parahuman fall. This may manifest in increased defensive modifiers if the mover was hit in the last round, an escape option that activates when the parahuman is about to die, a minor power that comes to light when more than half the wound slots are filled, or the ability to suppress wounds in order to run away.
Shaker: Temperance. The parahumans power becomes wider reaching over time. The longer the Shakers effect is active, the more dangerous it becomes, ramping up in damage, accuracy, range, etc. Alternatively, the power attempts to keep the parahuman up at all costs, possibly opening the option to decrease the power of the effect to heal them.
Stranger:
Temperance. The shard refuses to let the stranger fall. The power may have a safety switch that activates on a failed consciousness check by 1, or in a situation where the stranger may die. With this ability, the stranger may attempt to debilitate foes or hide once again in order to escape a losing situation.
Alternatively, the power could suppress and exchange wound effects that the stranger is suffering from for ones that are easier to deal with.
Striker:
Temperance.  Tenacity.  After every 2nd round of striking, the striker’s power gets a small boost, extending damage, accuracy against one of the defensive modes, effectiveness, environmental effects, etc.  Left free to keep delivering strikes, they get more and more dangerous.  The nature of the bonus that they ramp up with is decided as this is taken.  After 5 minutes of inactivity, they lose one step of boost.

Alternatively, every four rounds of striking against hostile threats regenerates the striker somewhat, keeping them in the fight
Tinker:
Temperance.  Pick one: two-handed guns have grenade launchers attached for easier and longer-range placement of grenades (and a grenade is added to List C), guns don’t need reloading, or range increments are extended 25’.

Alternatively, start with a ranged weapon built.  Either add ranged weapons to list of available builds or ranged weapons that can be built can have another pattern applied to them, switching from one pattern to the other with the press of a button (max once per 2 rounds).
Thinker:
Temperance.  Tenacity.  As combat progresses, the thinker builds mental charges that can be spent to expand their power, or granting bonuses such as increased damage, chance to hit, bonuses to remain in the fight, a single conditional reroll etc.  There is a cap on the number of charges, determined initially and constant thereafter.

Alternatively, every third round of combat, the thinker may suppress a number of wounds and their associated penalties.  This does not heal the wounds, merely allow the cape to press forwards in spite of their injuries

General:
Upright: Shadow self, attachment, addiction, restriction

Reversed:  Detachment, breaking free, power reclaimed
Blaster:
Devil.  The blaster can push their power past the limits the manton limit would normally allow.  In the wake of their power’s use, their hands shake with pain and they’re left gritting their teeth, flesh seared or damaged with veins of power slowly receding.  They gain one of the options below, but use of said option means they suffer a lesser wound and there are lingering signs they used their power until six hours pass or they get medical care.  If they already have a lesser wound, it advances to moderate, and if they’re full up on moderate wounds, they suffer a critical wound instead (effectively regeneration in reverse).  The wound comes with no effect.  The options are (choice made once, locked in):

●  Rolling explosion.  Unleash raw energies.  All foes within 15’ must pass a defensive roll or suffer moderate damage and the effects of the blaster’s blast element, while being pushed away to the edge of the effect.  Foes that pass their defensive rolls suffer a lesser wound instead.
●  Tap into other options at a cost of harming oneself.  Use blaster’s choice (choice made mid-combat, can choose different options each time) of the secondary blaster attack options.  The blast does more damage than usual.
●  Tap into other energies at a cost of harming oneself.  Use a different (but somewhat related) blaster element.  Three options are usually given at the time of this option being taken.  Further, the environmental effect is exaggerated in area and intensity.
●  Go breaker.  Gain some tertiary benefits relating to the element, damage taken is taken in the form of ability damage (lesser = 1 point of damage, moderate = 2), and gain some minor breaker powers, like floating or added attack or movement capabilities.  Damage is postponed until the breaker effect ends, and is delivered once per round the parahuman maintained the breaker effect.  Said damage cannot kill the parahuman, but can knock them out.
Breaker: Devil.  The cape has discovered how to further push their altered form towards offense.  This allows them to increase their speed, accuracy, or damage at the cost of losing most and or all of their defensive benefits, or opening up vulnerabilities where no defensive options existed.  Recovery of standard defensive benefits requires discarding the offensive bonus, as a standard action.
Brute:
Devil.  The brute has gained an understanding of how the power is distributed across their body.  They can shift the bias more toward offence, at the cost of defence.

Should they do so, they gain more raw offensive capability or unleash a one-use effect, but their defenses are hampered, being reduced in efficacy.  Either the cooldown to recover defenses is extended, layers of defenses are stripped away, a share of bonuses or stats are reduced (often 5% or 1 stat point), or (if using the models in the example brutes & brute generation below) the defensive aspect is weakened in the same way it might be to make room for a point of deviation.

However, while so limited, they can do one of the following (choice made once and locked in):
Manifest the offensive aspect of their defensive brute power for the duration.  Gain +2 Brawn or double existing Brawn bonus for purposes of feats of strength, while any melee/thrown weapon blows knock foes 5’ back per point they lost the defensive roll by.  Gain two of the limited use abilities: the second [Empress] intimidation ability, above, the [Chariot] leap, or any of the [Justice] charge abilities.
As part of this action, or while the defenses are so stripped, can elect to sustain half of their remaining health in moderate wounds (treat any fractions as lesser, deals minimum 1 moderate wound) to double the efficacy of the bonus.  This lasts 1d3+1 rounds.  The wounds are healed as per normal.

Recovery of standard defensive benefits requires first that the offensive boost is discarded, and is of moderate difficulty, counting from that point.
Changer: Devil.  The cape has discovered how to further push their altered form towards offense.  This allows them to increase their speed, accuracy, or damage at the cost of losing most and or all of their defensive benefits, or opening up vulnerabilities where no defensive options existed.  Recovery of standard defensive benefits requires discarding the offensive bonus, as a standard action.
Master: Devil.  The master has found a way to push their power to their limit.  For a master with a single construct, this allows them to make a single overcharged attack.  For masters with multiple constructs, the master is able to execute a combo attack, where some or all of their minions are able to work together for a single, higher-power all-or-nothing attack.  Use of this power harms the user, causing them damage or another penalty, or risks dispelling the minion(s), adding to the difficulty of creating new minions or imposing difficulty where none existed.
Mover: Devil. The parahuman may push past the point where their manton limits would usually apply. In the wake of their power’s use, their hands shake with pain and they’re left gritting their teeth, flesh seared or damaged with veins of power slowly receding. They gain the option of increasing their offensive potential at the risk of self-damage, permanent physical or mental backlash, or something else befitting their power. May even allow the parahuman to enter a breaker state as their human limits recede.
Shaker: Devil. The parahuman can push their power past the limits the manton limit would normally allow.  In the wake of their power’s use, their hands shake with pain and they’re left gritting their teeth, flesh seared or damaged with veins of power slowly receding.  They gain the option to increase their power at the risk of self damaging, pushing it out further, or with more aggression, or with more long lasting effects. May even allow the parahuman to go breaker as waves of power wash over their transformed body.
Stranger: Devil. Over time, the stranger has learnt to concentrate their power towards offense at the cost of their defensive capability or their deceptiveness. A shadow manipulator may draw their cloak of darkness into a weapon at the expense of their evasion bonus, for example. Returning to their normal state requires an action, condition, or there is a time limit to how long the stranger can maintain their offensive bent.
Striker:
Devil.  The striker can push their power past the limits the manton limit would normally allow.  In the wake of their power’s use, their hands shake with pain and they’re left gritting their teeth, flesh seared or damaged with veins of power slowly receding.  They gain one of the options below, but use of said option means they suffer a moderate wound and there are lingering signs they used their power until six hours pass or they get medical care.  If they’re full up on moderate wounds, they suffer a critical wound instead (effectively regeneration in reverse).  The wound comes with no effect.  The options are (choice made once, locked in):

●  Rolling explosion.  Unleash raw energies.  All foes within 15’ must pass a defensive roll or suffer moderate damage and the effects of the striker’s element, while being pushed away to the edge of the effect.  Foes that pass their defensive rolls suffer a lesser wound instead.
●  Tap into a reserve of power for their primary striker effect.  Dramatically amplifies the effect for a single attack.
●  Tap into other energies at a cost of harming oneself.  Use a different (but somewhat related) striker element.  Two options are usually given at the time of this option being taken.  Further, the environmental effect is exaggerated in area and intensity.
●  Enter a breaker state.  Gain some tertiary benefits relating to the element, damage taken is taken in the form of ability damage (1 point for a lesser, 2 points for a moderate and 3 for a critical), and gain some minor breaker powers, like floating or added attack or movement capabilities.  Damage is postponed until the breaker effect ends, and is delivered once per round the parahuman maintained the breaker effect.  Said damage cannot kill the parahuman, but can knock them out.
Tinker: Devil.  The tinker can overclock their gear, advancing its capabilities while introducing misfire chances.  Doing so can be done in advance, or modifications can be made with a round in combat.
Thinker: Devil.  The thinker has found a way to push their power to the edge of its capabilities, pushing the power and their mind into overdrive.  This gives them access to a powerful tool, enhancing their main power or granting secondary applications, but the use of this tool is costly and has guaranteed consequences.  Use of this power harms the user or causes them some other penalty, potentially making it harder to draw on their power, or increasing consequences from thinker headaches.

General:
Upright: Disaster, upheaval, sudden change, revelation

Reversed: Avoiding disaster, fear of change
Blaster:
Tower.  On a die roll of 12+, bypass and destroy target’s cover.  If the blast would already bypass cover, it instead does so catastrophically, causing those who were benefitting from it to stagger.

Alternately, determine two additional blaster elements that relate to the blaster’s first.  On delivering a blast, they can choose which element kicks in for environmental effect, and they gain some utility in that they can tap into these effects to alter their environment, typically delivered with touch.  A snow blaster might gain a wet slush and ice crystal effect, their snowballs drenching foes or congealing into large ice crystals on the struck target (or creating heaps of snow, as per normal).  They could also produce moisture at will and touch a door to freeze it shut.
Breaker:
Tower.  The Transformed state scales in magnitude over time.  Physical states add more raw mass, energy-based and esoteric states may surround the parahuman with a nimbus of energy that increases their effective scale while allowing them to float or move more easily.  They gain 5’ of reach and strike any opponent leaving their reach without shifting.  Increased size may cause them to suffer a -1 to dodges.

If they already have reach, they become huge, with an extra wound slot, they move 150% as far, and they can respond/retaliate to any non-free action activity within 10’ of them, and successful hits stagger the targets/interrupt the movement or action in question.  On the downside, they suffer -2 to defensive rolls, get half benefit from cover, and may be restricted or confounded by terrain that others aren’t; any space a 10’ diameter sphere wouldn’t pass through is considered impassable (but doorways can be broken, etc), and spaces a 10’ diameter sphere would scrape through are rough & hampers movement.

In regards to the huge size, either the boost is temporary and the parahuman chooses if and when they become huge, or it’s a part of the cape’s powers from this point on.
Brute:
Tower.  The Brute can scale up their size as they draw on their power.  Physical powers add more raw mass, energy-based and esoteric defenses may surround the parahuman with a nimbus of energy that increases their effective scale while allowing them to float or move more easily.  They gain 5’ of reach, strike first if charged, and strike any opponent leaving their reach, with a ‘roll an additional dice and keep highest result’ effect for any bash effects delivered.  They suffer a -1 to dodges.

If they already have reach, they become huge, with +33% to their total number of wounds, they move 150% as far, and they can respond/retaliate to any non-free action activity within 10’ of them, and successful hits stagger the targets/interrupt the movement or action in question.  On the downside, they suffer -2 to defensive rolls, get half benefit from cover, and may be restricted or confounded by terrain that others aren’t; any space a 10’ diameter sphere wouldn’t pass through is considered impassable (but doorways can be broken, etc), and spaces a 10’ diameter sphere would scrape through are rough & hampers movement.

In regards to the huge size, either the boost is temporary & the parahuman chooses if and when they become huge, or it’s a part of the brute powers being active from this point on, and the GM can choose if they reduce the defensive roll drawback to -1, get full benefit from cover, or if they’re not restricted by terrain.
Changer:
Tower.  The Transformed state scales in magnitude over time.  Physical states add more raw mass, energy-based and esoteric states may surround the parahuman with a nimbus of energy that increases their effective scale while allowing them to float or move more easily.  They gain 5’ of reach and strike any opponent leaving their reach without shifting.  Increased size may cause them to suffer a -1 to dodges.

If they already have reach, they become huge, with an extra wound slot, they move 150% as far, and they can respond/retaliate to any non-free action activity within 10’ of them, and successful hits stagger the targets/interrupt the movement or action in question.  On the downside, they suffer -2 to defensive rolls, get half benefit from cover, and may be restricted or confounded by terrain that others aren’t; any space a 10’ diameter sphere wouldn’t pass through is considered impassable (but doorways can be broken, etc), and spaces a 10’ diameter sphere would scrape through are rough & hampers movement.

In regards to the huge size, either the boost is temporary and the parahuman chooses if and when they become huge, or it’s a part of the cape’s powers from this point on.
Master: Tower.  The Master’s constructs grow in size.  This could be represented as more wound slots, the heavy quality, reach or the like, but may also cause them to be easier to hit as a consequence.
Mover:
Tower. The mover gains a destructive bent to their power, indiscriminate in effect. They may leave a deadly trail of power as they move, or have a minor striker power that destroys terrain and structures they touch, or something else as fits their power.

As an alternative option, the mover gains finer control over their own body’s kinesthesis through use of their power, as appropriate. A teleporter may be able to create portals for different parts of their body to reach farther, for example.
Shaker:
Tower. The Shaker’s power latches on to enemies who remain within it for too long. Those who find themselves suffering under the shakers sway may find themselves weakened or easier targets even after leaving it. 

Alternatively, the Shaker gains a more fine amount of control over their element, as appropriate.  A ice-based shaker might gain a wet slush and ice crystal effect, their snowstorm drenching foes or congealing into large ice crystals on the struck target (or creating heaps of snow, as per normal).  They could also produce moisture at will and touch a door to freeze it shut.
Stranger:
Tower. The stranger gains a violent, destructive scaling to their power, allowing their power to be twisted from subtle manipulation or cloaking into direct offense. A nimbus of energy that lashes out at those around, a dash ability that damages terrain, something along those lines.
Alternatively, the stranger gains a more fine amount of control over their element, as appropriate. A light-based stranger may be able to weave fine illusions over handheld objects, where they could only apply human-sized disguises before, or be able to create more intricate light illusions with greater detail - higher resolution, in a sense.
Striker:
Tower.  Striker builds up energy as they strike targets.  Each charge developed (after x successful attacks) can be spent on a future attack to add a small amount of extra range to it, as the energy is released.

Alternatively, the striker gains a more fine amount of control over their striker element, as appropriate.  A ice-based striker might gain a wet slush and ice crystal effect, their snowballs drenching foes or congealing into large ice crystals on the struck target (or creating heaps of snow, as per normal).  They could also produce moisture at will and touch a door to freeze it shut.
Tinker:
Tower.  When researching items, also idly produce random workstations and terminals in course of undertaking work.  Chance of building a terminal strongly depends on the size of the project, with masterprojects having a very large chance, and trinkets having negligible chance.  If tinker already has several workstations, instead gain progress toward upgrading one of them to an advanced variant.  Effectively advances a top-notch tinker workshop in the course of undertaking normal projects.  Long-term focused.

Alternatively, starts with a utility item offering some manipulation of environment (ie.  forcefield generator) or means of generating charges.  These items are available on the tinker’s list (expanding or adding to available capabilities) and are fractionally better.  Short-term focused.
Thinker: Tower.  The shard grants the thinker more awareness, allowing them to aim attacks at specific parts at no penalty.  Althernatively, gain a defensive reroll on a long cooldown, or only available in specific conditions.

General:
Upright: Hope, spirituality, renewal, serenity

Reversed: Lack of faith, despair, discouragement
Blaster:
Star.  The character gets a minor secondary power in another classification, one of the subtler disciplines: Stranger, Changer, Tinker, or Thinker.  The power typically touches on the blaster’s element.

Use of this secondary power requires warming up, and has a 50% chance of failure (oft with headaches or another status effect, while being disabled for 15 minutes to an hour, depending on the ambient conflict) if they aren’t in combat and haven’t used their blast already.

Other options for drawbacks include locking out the blast for 1d4 rounds or causing a wound to the user on a rolled 1 (rolls are presumed involved in this case).
Breaker: Star.  The character gets a minor secondary power in another classification, one of the subtler disciplines: Stranger, Changer, Tinker, or Thinker. Use of this secondary power is limited, with an hour long delay before it can be used again, if it is used outside of combat/conflict situations. The power typically touches on or relates the cape’s typing, power, or element. Use of this secondary power is limited, with moderate cooldowns if used in combat or only being usable once in each out of combat encounter.

Star. The character has a minor secondary power in another classification, drawing on one of the subtler disciplines; Stranger, Changer, or Tinker. The power relies on the shaker power being used first or in tandem.

Use of this secondary power is limited, or requires warming up
Stranger: Star. The stranger gets a minor secondary power in another classification, drawing on one of the subtler disciplines; Changer, Thinker, Tinker. The power typically touches on or relates the cape’s typing, power, or element. Use of this secondary power is limited, with moderate cooldowns if used in combat or only being usable once in each out of combat encounter.

The power typically touches on the striker’s typing and power.  Use of this secondary power is limited, with an hour long delay before it can be used again, if it is used outside of combat/conflict situations.  Used in combat situations, has a moderate recovery period.
Tinker:
Star.  The character has a minor secondary power in another classification, drawing on one of the subtler disciplines; Stranger, Changer, or Thinker.  The power may touch on the tinker’s specialization, and is more minor than most, often with a chance of misfiring or not working unless specific gear is made with an effort to encourage or channel the power (this is a modification to costume, one point in value).

Alternately, the tinker opens up a field of specialty touching on one of the aforementioned classifications (Oft relating known branches/specialties with Shroud, Ego, Psyche or Data).  Research and building in this peripheral area is 25% slower, research and building in prior areas is sped up.
Thinker: Star.  The character has a minor secondary power in another classification, drawing on one of the subtler disciplines; Stranger, Changer, or Tinker.  This power relies on the thinker power, which must typically be used first or in tandem with the secondary.  Use of this secondary power is limited, causing an immediate thinker headache if it is used outside of combat/conflict situations.  Used in combat situations, has a moderate recovery period.

Use of this secondary power is limited, with an hour long delay before it can be used again, if it is used outside of combat/conflict situations.  Used in combat situations, has a moderate recovery period.

Tinker:
Moon.  The character has a minor secondary power in another classification, drawing on one of the wider-reaching disciplines; Shaker, Brute, Mover, or Master.  The power may touch on the tinker’s specialization, and is more minor than most, often with a chance of misfiring or not working unless specific gear is made with an effort to encourage or focus the power (this is a modification to costume, one point in value).

Alternately, the tinker opens up a field of specialty touching on one of the aforementioned classifications (Oft relating known branches/specialties with Travel, Life, Impulse, or Growth).  Research and building in this peripheral area is 25% slower, research and building in prior areas is sped up.
Thinker: Moon.  The character gets a minor secondary power in another classification, one of the wider-reaching disciplines: Shaker, Blaster, Mover, or Master.  This power relies on the thinker power, which must typically be used first or in tandem with the secondary.  Use of this secondary power is limited, causing an immediate thinker headache if it is used outside of combat/conflict situations.  Used in combat situations, has a moderate recovery period.

General:
Upright:  Judgement, rebirth, inner calling, absolution

Reversed: Self doubt, refusal to look inward
Blaster:
Judgement.  The blaster sheds some of their human limits.  Pick two:
●  They gain some degree of nourishment from whatever their element is.  Pyrokinetics might gain food from heat sources.  An emotion blaster might draw nourishment from human misery.  So long as the food source is available, the blaster spends a little less on an ongoing basis, and is considered to be having top-notch meals all the time, for morale tiebreakers.
●  Manton limits regarding the blaster’s element are improved, or are extended to include related elements, well beyond the usual.  Fire resistance might now grant immunity, or become more about ‘energy’ and encompass electrical damage.  Extent of improvement depends on how common the element is.
●  The shard has a higher active role in the blaster’s mind; negative mental effects that would be instilled have a 50% chance of failing.  Further, Parahumans attempting to dig into the parahuman’s mind or emotions or using other senses to track the parahuman may suffer a backlash, often themed around the element.  Severity of backlash depends on intimacy and directness of the read.  Ambient clairvoyance that includes the parahuman in the area might lead to a cape feeling colder by the second.  Reading the cape’s emotions or memories might deliver a lesser wound in addition to chilling the prying cape.  Physical effects are purely psychosomatic/hallucination and fade after rest.  Being affected in this way informs nearby individuals that something is really weird with the prying cape.
●  The shard will try to keep the blaster alive.  When the Blaster is on the brink of death and fails their Guts check, they collapse and enter a hibernation state.  They do not die unless finished off.  Recharges after 24 hours.
●  The blaster’s lowest stat gains a stat point.
●  The blaster’s highest stat gains a stat point.  Determine randomly in case of tie.  Cannot be taken with the other stat point.
Breaker: Judgement.  The cape gains a quality of life improvement, eliminating the need for food, rest and sleep.  Cape becomes far more durable, gaining +2 to all guts-based rolls against poisons and the like, as well as all guts-based willpower rolls including consciousness.  Additionally, if the cape would die, they instead revert to their unconscious human form and they do not die unless finished off.  Recharges after 24 hours.
Brute:
Judgement.  The Brute gains a quality of life improvement, eliminating the need for food, water and sleep while they gain a partial immunity to manton effects, becoming resistant to effects that target organics only; powers that don’t allow dice rolls now allow them.

Alternately, they gain a bonus in a realm respective to their subcategory:

●  Armor, Muscle, or Shield brutes could gain +3 physical stat points, for noncombat purposes and feats of strength only.
●  Intensity, Field, and Dynamic brutes gradually change the environment around them as they fight, warping reality (ambient temperature gradually increases/decreases, moisture collects & runs down walls); grants a small attritional, element-based environment in combat and allows them to make their headquarters or place of rest a ‘attacking Russia in the wintertime’ proposition.  Manton benefits extend further.
●  Sunder, Repression, and Negate brutes grant a small resistance to parahuman effects that are not directly targeting them or that same resistance to effects that do directly target them.
●  Regen and Transfiguration Brutes recover faster after altercations, and if they finish a fight without needing to draw on all of their recovery power, they gain a bonus to non-physical stats (ie.  Social, Know, Wits) or multiple perks, solely for non-combat actions and only until their next combat.  Bonus scales with how much recovery was used.  A cape that can recover from death that didn’t die might get the full bonus, as would a regenerator who doesn’t get hurt.  The bonus diminishes with successive uses and can be conserved.
●  Immortal brutes can choose to gain three milestones of the general type of their choosing (potentially rolling thrice on this chart) and stop aging altogether, but gained milestones from that point on are a single option among recruits, swell ranks, resources and interest.

Master:
Judge.  The master sheds some of their human limits.  Pick two:

●  The Master’s highest stat gains a stat point.  Determine randomly in case of tie.  Cannot be taken with the other stat point.

Tinker:
Judgement.  The existence of a tinker is a high-maintenance one, with heavy demands for time, resources, and to have one’s own gear on hand during a crisis.  With all of these little drawbacks, the tinker has found a way to put them to rest.  Pick one:
1.  The tinker has found a way to avoid needing to sleep.  More tinker time available per day.
2.  The tinker has found a way avoid needing to eat/maintain hygiene.  Somewhat more tinker time available per day.
3.  The tinker has found a way to access their gear, teleporting it to them, keeping the costume in a subspace package the size of a brick, or some such.  Accessing their gear is quick.
4.  The tinker has shored up mortal weaknesses and either augments a stat by one point or takes a one-point costume quality and appends it to their physiology.
The first one is free.  Either the second one is free but is accessed with the tinker’s next milestone, or the tinker can take multiple, with a requirement of 4 time slots & $5000 for the second (first remains free) and the amount doubling for each thereafter.  Each can only be taken once.  Time slots invested can be broken up over multiple terms and need not be consecutive.
The mechanism by which these things are achieved may involve technology that is built into the tinker, which could be damaged if suffering a mortal wound (torso/head) or break (limb), or it could involve a need for regular intake of drugs or power.
